title: Correct active transaction rebuild and resume selection behavior
summary: Fix the rebuild path so the materialized transaction state consistently selects the correct active transaction from the canonical event log during resume. This ticket focuses on correctly handling later tx.begin events after terminal transactions, preventing misleading last_applied_seq snapshots when active_tx selection is stale, and adding regression coverage for active transaction rebuild behavior.
outputs: Rebuild logic that materializes the latest active transaction consistently from the canonical event log
Correct resume selection behavior when later tx.begin events follow terminal transactions
Regression coverage for stale active transaction rebuild and cross-session active transaction selection
acceptance_criteria: Rebuild logic correctly materializes the latest active transaction when tx.end.done or tx.end.blocked is followed by a later tx.begin.
The system does not write or preserve a tx_state snapshot whose last_applied_seq appears current while active_tx content is stale, empty, or inconsistent with the latest canonical events.
Resume selection prefers the latest non-terminal transaction even when multiple sessions appear in the canonical event log.
Terminal transactions do not incorrectly suppress a later active transaction during rebuild.
Regression tests cover stale active transaction rebuilds, incorrect no-active-transaction fallback, and latest-active transaction selection for the observed failure modes.
tasks: Review state_rebuilder and related state materialization paths to identify how active transaction selection can drift from the latest canonical event sequence.
Fix rebuild handling for terminal events followed by a later tx.begin so the later transaction becomes active reliably.
Ensure latest non-terminal transaction selection remains deterministic across sessions.
Tighten integrity handling so last_applied_seq cannot advance without active_tx and related lifecycle state being updated consistently.
Add or extend regression tests covering rebuild correctness, active transaction handoff, and latest-active transaction selection.
notes: This ticket is focused on rebuild correctness and resume selection behavior, not on relaxing workflow guards.
Prefer explicit integrity failures over silent fallback to a default no-active-transaction state when canonical events indicate an active transaction should exist.
Keep active transaction selection deterministic even when multiple session identifiers appear in the canonical event log.
Clarify the ticket focus around rebuild correctness and latest-active transaction selection rather than broader observability scope.
